threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it ‘the Tube’ instead of ‘the Overground’ when you’re just a stop away from Dalston Junction.
Last night on the 59, a bloke serenaded the whole bus with opera. Thought it was mad until he hit a high note and even the driver chuckled.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was home? Hearing a busker play a blend of Turkish pop and Berliner Schlager on the corner of Weserstraße. Pure Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you catch a bus that arrives late because it's stuck in traffic from a street festival—while the festival is still happening.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know you’re local when you spot the taco truck lineup before the tourists do. They think they’re eating gourmet, but we know better.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 6 at Civic Center today; we sat for 15 minutes because a guy decided to perform a solo concert on the tracks. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always think Gràcia is just about Gaudí, but the best bars are the ones tucked away on the side streets—like the one behind the Sant Joan church.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their confusion at the ferreteria; locals know it’s not a hardware store but a place for eclectic finds.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still hesitate when the bus driver yells 'next stop' because you think it’s just a polite suggestion.
Caught the 59 last night; two guys started a full-on debate about the best jollof rice in Brixton—had me grinning all the way to Norwood.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a group of seniors playing cards in the park, arguing passionately over who cheated last time. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you stumble upon a silent disco in an old factory courtyard, but be ready for spontaneous pop-up performances from locals.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they’ve accepted an 8-minute Muni delay as basically on time—but freak out if there’s even a hint of dog on the tracks.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni at Civic Center today, and a dog was just lounging on the tracks like it owned the place. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just grab a takeaway coffee and stroll, but locals know that sitting at a terrace is the whole point.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still look baffled trying to navigate the Gràcia streets; locals know it's all about the shortcuts through the plaça and the alleys.
